issues: Headline is fabricated/AI-generated — no headline is visible in the OCR text for this article. The draft presents a plausible but unverified headline.
Draft description omits Jamie Stewart, who played for Langley and Abbotsford and is a known Sockeyes roster member — he should be highlighted in the description.
The OCR also references a 'Harrison' who last played for Kelowna Packers, but no first name is given — the draft omits this person entirely. Not a Sockeyes member but worth noting for accuracy.
The long description correctly lists the former Sockeyes (Wensley, Major, King, Tuttle, Rutherford) but fails to call out Jamie Stewart explicitly as a Sockeyes player listed in the same article.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Article listing BCJHL graduates playing U.S. college hockey, including former Richmond Sockeyes Paul Rutherford, Jamie Stewart, and others.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Article highlights BCJHL graduates competing in U.S. college hockey for the 1987–88 season. Former Richmond Sockeyes named include David Wensley, Bruce Major, Scott King, Steve Tuttle, Paul Rutherford, and Jamie Stewart (who also played for Langley and Abbotsford). Other graduates mentioned come from Kelowna, Penticton, Vernon, Nanaimo, Shuswap, and Bowling Green, including goalies Frank Furlan and Al Loring of Nanaimo.
review.notes: No headline is present in the OCR — the AI-drafted headline should be cleared. Jamie Stewart is on the confirmed Sockeyes roster and appears in the OCR; the draft's long description omitted him as a Sockeyes connection. David Wensley and Bruce Major are not on the provided Sockeyes roster list — editor should confirm whether they were also Sockeyes alumni.
